title: vernonia calvoana var. mokaensis
intro: Vernonia calvoana var. mokaensis is a plant species classified as a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Traditionally, Vernonia calvoana var. mokaensis has been used in medicinal applications due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The plant has been reported to possess several health benefits, including its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. Vernonia calvoana var. mokaensis is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and the subsequent consumption for therapeutic purposes.
